title_en: Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani: The Sufi Hagiographer and Hadith Master
title_ar: أبو نعيم الأصفهاني: المحدث وكاتب السير
content_en:
Ahmad ibn Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Ishaq al-Isfahani, known as Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani (336–430 AH / 948–1038 CE), was one of the most prolific and accomplished hadith scholars of his generation, combining an extraordinary mastery of the hadith sciences with a deep personal commitment to the Sufi spiritual path. Born in Isfahan in Persia, he lived and taught there for most of his century-long life, making his city a major center of hadith scholarship in the 4th–5th centuries AH.

Abu Nu'aym's scholarly formation placed him at the intersection of two powerful intellectual and spiritual currents: the hadith transmission tradition that valued the careful preservation and authentication of prophetic reports, and the emerging Sufi tradition that sought to articulate the inner dimensions of Islamic spirituality and document the lives of those who had achieved exceptional nearness to God. His great work Hilyat al-Awliya wa Tabaqat al-Asfiya (The Adornment of the Saints and the Ranks of the Pure) synthesizes these two currents: it is simultaneously a biographical dictionary of Islamic spiritual figures and a hadith compilation, presenting the hadith transmissions and spiritual teachings of its subjects alongside their biographical information.

His command of the hadith corpus was exceptional: he is said to have memorized and transmitted hundreds of thousands of hadith, making him one of the major transmission authorities for several important hadith collections. His Musnad al-Imam Abi Hanifa is an important early work. His Ma'rifat as-Sahaba is a significant early work on companion biography. His Tib al-Mawlud (The Medicine of the Newborn) and Dala'il an-Nubuwwa (Proofs of Prophethood) represent other contributions to Islamic scholarship.

Hilyat al-Awliya is Abu Nu'aym's most substantial and most read work, running to ten volumes in modern editions. It begins with the companions and progresses through successive generations of Islamic spiritual masters (the 'layers' or tabaqat of the title) up to his own time, presenting the life, teachings, and spiritual states of each as a model of Islamic holiness. The work is a foundational text for the history of Islamic spirituality and Sufism.
title_id: Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani: Ahli Hadis dan Penulis Sejarah Para Wali
content_id:
Ahmad ibn Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Ishaq al-Isfahani, yang dikenal sebagai Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani (336–430 H / 948–1038 M), adalah salah satu ulama hadis yang paling produktif dan berprestasi dari generasinya, memadukan penguasaan luar biasa atas ilmu-ilmu hadis dengan komitmen pribadi yang mendalam terhadap jalan spiritual tasawuf. Lahir di Isfahan, Persia, ia hidup dan mengajar di sana hampir sepanjang hidupnya yang berlangsung hampir satu abad, menjadikan kotanya sebagai pusat besar keilmuan hadis pada abad ke-4 hingga ke-5 H.

Pembentukan intelektual Abu Nu'aym menempatkannya di persimpangan dua arus intelektual dan spiritual yang kuat: tradisi transmisi hadis yang menghargai pelestarian dan autentifikasi yang cermat atas riwayat-riwayat kenabian, serta tradisi tasawuf yang sedang berkembang yang berusaha mengartikulasikan dimensi-dimensi batin spiritualitas Islam dan mendokumentasikan kehidupan orang-orang yang telah mencapai kedekatan luar biasa dengan Allah. Karya agungnya Hilyat al-Awliya' wa Tabaqat al-Asfiya' menyintesiskan dua arus ini: ia sekaligus merupakan kamus biografi tokoh-tokoh spiritual Islam dan kompilasi hadis, yang menyajikan transmisi-transmisi hadis dan ajaran-ajaran spiritual dari subjek-subjeknya bersama informasi biografis mereka.

Penguasaannya atas korpus hadis sangat luar biasa: ia dikatakan telah menghafal dan mentransmisikan ratusan ribu hadis, menjadikannya salah satu otoritas transmisi utama untuk beberapa koleksi hadis penting. Musnad al-Imam Abi Hanifa-nya adalah karya awal yang penting. Ma'rifat as-Shahaba-nya adalah karya awal yang signifikan tentang biografi sahabat. Thibb al-Mawlud (Pengobatan Bayi yang Baru Lahir) dan Dala'il an-Nubuwwa (Bukti-Bukti Kenabian) mewakili kontribusi-kontribusi lainnya bagi keilmuan Islam.

Hilyat al-Awliya' adalah karya Abu Nu'aym yang paling substansial dan paling banyak dibaca, mencapai sepuluh jilid dalam cetakan modern. Ia dimulai dengan para sahabat dan berlanjut melalui generasi-generasi berikutnya dari para master spiritual Islam (the 'layers' atau tabaqat dari judul tersebut) hingga zamannya sendiri, menyajikan kehidupan, ajaran, dan keadaan spiritual masing-masing sebagai model kesucian Islam. Karya ini merupakan teks fondasi bagi sejarah spiritualitas Islam dan tasawuf.
content_ar:
Ahmad ibn Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Ishaq al-Isfahani, known as Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani (336–430 AH / 948–1038 CE), was one of the most prolific and accomplished الحديث scholars of his generation, combining an extraordinary mastery of the الحديث sciences with a deep personal commitment to the Sufi spiritual path. Born in Isfahan in Persia, he lived and taught there for most of his century-long life, making his city a major center of الحديث scholarship in the 4th–5th centuries AH.

Abu Nu'aym's scholarly formation placed him at the intersection of two powerful intellectual and spiritual currents: the الحديث transmission tradition that valued the careful preservation and authentication of prophetic reports, and the emerging Sufi tradition that sought to articulate the inner dimensions of Islamic spirituality and document the lives of those who had achieved exceptional nearness to God. His great work Hilyat al-Awliya wa Tabaqat al-Asfiya (The Adornment of the Saints and the Ranks of the Pure) synthesizes these two currents: it is simultaneously a biographical dictionary of Islamic spiritual figures and a الحديث compilation, presenting the الحديث transmissions and spiritual teachings of its subjects alongside their biographical information.

His command of the الحديث corpus was exceptional: he is said to have memorized and transmitted hundreds of thousands of الحديث, making him one of the major transmission authorities for several important الحديث collections. His Musnad al-Imam Abi Hanifa is an important early work. His Ma'rifat as-Sahaba is a significant early work on companion biography. His Tib al-Mawlud (The Medicine of the Newborn) and Dala'il an-Nubuwwa (Proofs of Prophethood) represent other contributions to Islamic scholarship.

Hilyat al-Awliya is Abu Nu'aym's most substantial and most read work, running to ten volumes in modern editions. It begins with the الصحابة and progresses through successive generations of Islamic spiritual masters (the 'layers' or tabaqat of the title) up to his own time, presenting the life, teachings, and spiritual states of each as a model of Islamic holiness. The work is a foundational text for the history of Islamic spirituality and Sufism.
